course.title: Build and Ship a Production Static Site Generator with Python 3.14
course.description: Python, object-oriented programming, Git, and responsive-web learners who need to design, build, test, package, deploy, migrate, and defend a production static-site generator rather than copy a narrow Markdown replacement tutorial. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; static delivery and url models; import-safe cli and atomic pipeline; pathlib content admission; unicode and typed metadata; source-mapped asts; bounded inline and block parsing; recursion and recovery; semantic context-safe html rendering; jinja 3.1.6; slugs, permalinks, redirects, navigation, taxonomies, feeds, and sitemaps; content-addressed assets; dependency-aware incremental builds; wcag 2.2; responsive progressive enhancement; security; layered tests and validation; diagnostics and profiles; python packaging; reproducible ci and github pages deployment; migration, rollback, recovery, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate a deliberately bounded educational markdown subset compared against commonmark 0.31.2 and markdown-it-py 4.2.0, with honest conformance, raw-html, parser-complexity, browser, filesystem, template, validator, deployment, and hosting transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic publication deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-site defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves reader outcome, content authority, revision, Python and tool versions, source and route identity, trust policy, accessibility requirement, bounded-resource rule, changed fixture, failure evidence, recovery, and explicit browser-versus-production limits before adding one publishing boundary.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; those require controlled transfer evidence.

Passing work requires a stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate parse or graph state, exact observable output, a changed and rejected case, a test that detects a deliberate defect, accessible stakeholder evidence, and a named owner for remaining risk.

credential.title: Build and Ship a Production Static Site Generator with Python 3.14 Certificate

modules.title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence
modules.description: A team says “build a blog generator” but has not defined readers, publishing authority, content ownership, supported routes, accessibility needs, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Learners cumulatively produce reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.

modules.title: Static Delivery, HTTP Representations, URLs, Bases, and Origins
modules.description: Pages work from a local file but break below a project subpath, directories redirect unexpectedly, fragments point nowhere, and the team confuses filesystem paths with URLs. Learners cumulatively produce versioned URL and delivery contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.
Keep platform filesystem paths, POSIX publication paths, percent-encoded URL paths, queries, and fragments as distinct types.
Model root, project-subpath, preview, custom-domain, trailing-slash, and relative-link behavior under one explicit base URL.

modules.title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output
modules.description: The script reads arguments during import, mixes parsing with writes, partially replaces the public directory on failure, and cannot explain which configuration won. Learners cumulatively produce import-safe deterministic build pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.
Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.
Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.

modules.title: Pathlib, Content Discovery, Roots, Symlinks, and Admission
modules.description: Recursive discovery follows a symlink outside content, includes hidden drafts and generated output, treats directories as documents, and overwrites two sources onto one route. Learners cumulatively produce bounded content-discovery manifest through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.
Declare roots, extensions, ignores, hidden files, drafts, ordering, maximum files, depth, and total bytes before traversal.
Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.

modules.title: Bytes, Unicode, Newlines, Front Matter, Metadata, and Schemas
modules.description: The build relies on locale encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, parses arbitrary YAML objects, accepts duplicate metadata keys, and compares dates in mixed formats. Learners cumulatively produce versioned decoded-document and metadata contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.
Choose normalization, identifier, comparison, sorting, and display policies while retaining original author text.
Recognize bounded delimiters, separate metadata bytes from body, reject malformed closure, and avoid executing content.

modules.title: Tokens, Source Spans, Document Trees, Invariants, and Traversal
modules.description: The parser concatenates HTML strings immediately, loses source locations, uses one mutable node class for every construct, and cannot report which input caused invalid nesting. Learners cumulatively produce typed source-mapped document tree through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish scanning, tokenization, block parsing, inline parsing, tree construction, transformation, validation, and rendering.
Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.
Retain source path, line, column, byte or code-point span, and original excerpt through transformations.

modules.title: Inline Scanning, Delimiters, Escapes, Code Spans, and Emphasis
modules.description: A regex pairs the first and last asterisks, parses markup inside code, mishandles backslash escapes, loses literal delimiters, and becomes quadratic on long adversarial input. Learners cumulatively produce bounded inline tokenizer and delimiter resolver through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Advance one cursor through text, escapes, entities, code spans, delimiters, links, and literal fallbacks without dropping characters.
Match bounded backtick runs, normalize permitted interior whitespace, preserve code text, and reject missing closure predictably.
Classify delimiter runs from surrounding characters, track opener and closer state, and resolve nested emphasis deterministically.

modules.title: Links, Images, References, Autolinks, and URL Safety
modules.description: Nested brackets confuse destination parsing, unresolved references disappear, javascript URLs survive into output, duplicate labels change silently, and every image receives its filename as alt text. Learners cumulatively produce reference-aware accessible link and media model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Parse labels, destinations, optional titles, nesting, escaping, angle forms, and balanced delimiters under a named subset.
Normalize reference labels, detect duplicates, resolve forward references, preserve unresolved source, and report locations.
Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.

modules.title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code
modules.description: Blank lines erase structure, list markers nest by guesswork, headings skip source evidence, a fence closes with the wrong marker, and paragraphs swallow following blocks. Learners cumulatively produce line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent line content, indentation, blankness, offset, tab expansion, newline, and container prefix without discarding source positions.
Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.
Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.

modules.title: Recursive Containers, Stack Parsing, Depth, and Recovery
modules.description: Deeply nested lists overflow recursion, an unmatched container consumes the rest of the file, child offsets drift, and one invalid construct prevents later valid content from rendering. Learners cumulatively produce bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define base case, recursive reduction, returned state, source advancement, and invariant for nested document structures.
Use an explicit open-container stack where depth, continuation, closure, and source ownership need controlled iteration.
Reject or recover from excessive nesting before Python recursion, memory, or diagnostic output becomes unbounded.

modules.title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism
modules.description: Text nodes can inject markup, code is double-escaped, attributes are unquoted, heading IDs collide, dictionary order leaks into output, and raw HTML bypasses the trust model. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
Encode untrusted text and attribute values for their exact HTML context and reject unsafe script, style, comment, and URL placements.
Keep trusted renderer output distinct from untrusted author text and make every conversion into trusted markup explicit and reviewable.

modules.title: Jinja 3.1.6 Environments, Layouts, Autoescape, and Contracts
modules.description: Templates are constructed from author text, autoescape is assumed rather than configured, missing values render empty, safe filters spread, inheritance cycles hide layout ownership, and logic consumes arbitrary objects. Learners cumulatively produce strict autoescaped layout and component system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create one configured Jinja environment with bounded loader roots, explicit autoescape, StrictUndefined, deterministic globals, and version evidence.
Pass a documented immutable view model instead of filesystem objects, services, secrets, or unrestricted application state.
Use base layouts, blocks, includes, and macros with explicit ownership, required regions, naming, and bounded inheritance.

modules.title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests
modules.description: Titles become unstable routes, Unicode collapses into empty slugs, case-only paths collide on deployment, old links vanish after rename, and canonical URLs point at preview origins. Learners cumulatively produce collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.
Define Unicode normalization, transliteration or preservation, case, separators, reserved names, empties, length, and collision behavior.
Select explicit or derived permalinks, trailing-slash convention, date behavior, language prefix, and base path with migration evidence.

modules.title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps
modules.description: Navigation depends on filesystem order, tags differ by case, drafts leak into feeds, pagination duplicates pages, orphan content is invisible, and sitemap URLs disagree with canonical routes. Learners cumulatively produce reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.
Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.
Build global, local, breadcrumb, previous-next, related, and multiple-way navigation from an acyclic visible route graph.

modules.title: Assets, Media Admission, Fingerprints, CSS, and Integrity
modules.description: Assets are copied by basename, path traversal escapes the output root, CSS references are missed, hashes change with timestamps, huge images ship unchanged, and stale files survive rebuilds. Learners cumulatively produce bounded content-addressed asset manifest through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Admit assets by root, type, extension, signature where needed, size, dimensions, license, owner, and active-content policy.
Map source asset identity to collision-free public URL and output path without trusting basename or author traversal.
Hash canonical bytes with recorded algorithm and use the digest for cache-busting identity without embedding unstable metadata.

modules.title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode
modules.description: Changing a layout rebuilds one page, deleting content leaves output behind, cached nodes ignore parser versions, watch events duplicate builds, and a fast incremental result differs from a clean build. Learners cumulatively produce clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output.
Key cached stages by canonical input bytes, relevant configuration, algorithm and tool versions, schema, and platform boundary.
Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.

modules.title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language
modules.description: Templates emit multiple mains, heading levels follow visual size, skip links target missing IDs, repeated links have vague labels, code lacks context, and media alternatives are never reviewed. Learners cumulatively produce WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.

modules.title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets
modules.description: Generated pages assume one viewport, content clips at zoom, navigation depends on JavaScript, fonts block text, images shift layout, and a fast home page hides slow long-form routes. Learners cumulatively produce responsive progressively enhanced performance budget through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.
Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
Deliver core reading and navigation in HTML, layer CSS and optional JavaScript, and define failure behavior for blocked or broken enhancements.

modules.title: Threat Models, Untrusted Content, XSS, Paths, Templates, and CSP
modules.description: Raw HTML executes author input, javascript links survive, a template loader escapes its root, copied SVG contains active content, secrets enter output, and CSP is treated as the only XSS defense. Learners cumulatively produce explicit static-publishing trust and security policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.
Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.

modules.title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests
modules.description: Tests snapshot everything, approve changed HTML blindly, omit malformed input, compare only strings, never mutate fixtures, and treat validator success as user-flow proof. Learners cumulatively produce layered parser-to-browser verification matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.
Use small reviewed input, expected tree, diagnostics, route manifest, and output fixtures with explicit update review.
Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.

modules.title: Diagnostics, Build Reports, Metrics, Profiles, and Failure Triage
modules.description: The command prints unordered warnings without locations, hides skipped pages, reports only total duration, logs author content and local paths, and cannot compare a slow build with a prior release. Learners cumulatively produce bounded actionable build and publication evidence report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.
Sort deterministically, deduplicate, cap cascades, promote configured warnings, summarize omissions, and return status from policy.
Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.

modules.title: Project Metadata, Python Packaging, Entry Points, Plugins, and Clean Installs
modules.description: The generator runs only from its repository, imports templates through the current directory, has unbounded dependencies, discovers arbitrary plugins, omits package data, and is never installed into a clean environment. Learners cumulatively produce installed reproducible static-site command through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Declare build backend, project metadata, Python bounds, dependencies, optional groups, entry point, package data, license, and version source in pyproject.toml.
Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.
Load packaged templates, schemas, and default assets through supported resource APIs rather than current working directory assumptions.

modules.title: Reproducible Builds, CI Gates, Artifacts, GitHub Pages, and Promotion
modules.description: CI downloads floating tools, embeds current time, deploys from a dirty workspace, rebuilds after approval, grants broad token permissions, and publishes preview origins as canonical URLs. Learners cumulatively produce immutable reproducible staged deployment pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Eliminate unstable order, paths, locale, time, random values, permissions, and environment leakage; use recorded source and tool identity and compare clean digests.
Pin workflow dependencies, minimize permissions, isolate untrusted changes, protect environments, retain logs safely, and separate build from deployment authority.
Build once, validate, identify, attest, retain, approve, and deploy the exact same immutable artifact instead of rebuilding.

modules.title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense
modules.description: The newest artifact replaces production without a route diff, removed URLs are discovered by readers, rollback requires rebuilding, caches preserve broken assets, ownership ends after deployment, and generated volume is called completion. Learners cumulatively produce production publication, migration, and recovery dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.
Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.
Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.
